task_completion.description: Launch and navigate to the GCompris Submarine activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the Submarine activity (which features a submarine, water, and ballast controls) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Pilot the submarine to the ocean floor
task_completion.visual_evidence: The submarine is visually positioned at the very bottom of the water/ocean floor within the GCompris activity.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the submarine at the ocean floor
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool, save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows an image being saved as 'submarine_depth.png' in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Pilot the submarine to the surface of the water
task_completion.visual_evidence: The submarine is visually positioned at the top/surface of the water within the GCompris activity.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the submarine at the surface
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool, save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows an image being saved as 'submarine_surface.png' in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Write an explanation of the physics in captains_log.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation and editing of '~/Documents/captains_log.txt', containing a written explanation of buoyancy physics using required terms like ballast, density, sink, and rise.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris Submarine activity interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GCompris UI with varying water levels in the ballast tanks or different submarine positions over time, proving active gameplay rather than viewing static images.

integrity.description: The agent wrote an original explanation of the physics rather than just pasting the prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in captains_log.txt forms complete, coherent sentences explaining buoyancy principles, not just a disconnected list of the required keywords or a direct copy-paste of the task description.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files to the actual requested directory
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or save dialogs confirm the files are placed specifically in the '~/Documents/' directory, not just on the Desktop or a temporary folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The GCompris Submarine activity (found in the Science category) teaches buoyancy using ballast tank mechanics. The task requires two screenshots saved to ~/Documents/: 'submarine_depth.png' showing the submarine at the ocean floor, and 'submarine_surface.png' showing it at the surface. The 'captains_log.txt' file must contain a written explanation of buoyancy physics and must include at least two of the following keywords: ballast, density, sink, rise, float, tank, water, valve.
